title: Reuters: AI infrastructure debt is reshaping corporate bond markets
friendlyTitle: AI data centers are being funded with huge debt deals
summary: Reuters reports that AI-related debt is approaching 15% of U.S. investment-grade bond issuance this year, with Amazon and Alphabet issuing bonds across multiple currencies to fund AI infrastructure. Banks are also developing data-center lease-backed debt structures for AI and compute projects.
friendlySummary: Big technology companies are borrowing large amounts of money to build the chips, clouds, and data centers needed for AI. Banks are creating new financing methods for these projects.
whyItMatters: AI’s buildout is no longer just a venture-capital story; it is moving into mainstream credit markets and global infrastructure finance. That affects cloud pricing, data-center availability, energy demand, and the cost base behind everyday AI tools.
friendlyWhyItMatters: The cost of building AI affects the tools people use every day. More expensive infrastructure can shape prices, availability, and which companies can compete.
